what_it_does: This file is a channel attachment metadata record in YAML frontmatter that links an attachment ID to a design-source channel image target.
observations: file body is empty after frontmatter
standouts: Target uses an image:// URI scheme
Attachment record is a bare metadata stub with no embedded image data or alt text
concerns: Target path p-18 is opaque; no alt text, dimensions, or MIME type provided
No content beyond the reference, so the file cannot validate the asset
notes: A bare metadata stub linking attachment att-d8075f3c to image p-18 in the design-source channel.
questions: What does p-18 represent or depict?
Who added this attachment and why was design-source chosen?
Where is the actual image asset stored and how is it resolved?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is an images attachment, but the file only stores a metadata pointer and timestamp, not image content.
intention_signals: To create a durable, timestamped reference from a channel attachment to an external image asset.
novelty: Use of an image:// URI scheme for the target; otherwise a standard attachment stub.
contribution: Provides a canonical attachment identifier and a channel-scoped image reference with a creation timestamp for asset tracking.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter channel attachment record mapping att-d8075f3c to image target image://design-source/company-info/p-18 in the design-source channel with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To record when and where an image asset was attached to the design-source channel.
